--

In addition to the defaults of strict mode and es2025, TypeScript 6 has silently shipped a few features that will be useful (the most developers have not touched these yet).
Press enter or click to view image in full size

If you quickly skimmed the TypeScript 6 release notes up until “strict mode is now default” and then stopped, you are missing the juicy bits.
The shifting tsconfig defaults are another thing which many write-ups on TS6 are obsessed with. Alright, I hear you, that is the headline. But if you look under the covers, there are a handful of features that silently address problems I’ve been solving workarounds for year.
What should honestly be in your daily workflow.
1. Deploying for Resources That Will Actually Reclean Themselves
This one is for those among us who has forgotten to close a DB connection, or clear a listener. The first feature of TypeScript 6 is explicit resource management.
function readUserSession() {
using session = openSession(); // auto-disposed at scope end
const user = session.getUser();
return user.name;
} // session.dispose() called automatically hereEliminate try/finally boilerplate just to ensure cleanup It’s a tiny syntax change but huge reliability gain in anything…
